title: Work Center
learning_objectives: Understand the purpose of a Work Center.
Learn how Work Centers are used in production.
Identify important Work Center master data.
Understand capacity planning using Work Centers.
Learn standard SAP transaction codes for Work Centers.

introduction.title: What is a Work Center?
introduction.content: A Work Center represents the physical location where manufacturing operations are performed. It may represent a machine, production line, department, workstation, or a group of employees. Work Centers are used to define where operations are executed, calculate production capacity, scheduling, labor requirements and production costs.

business_purpose.title: Why is a Work Center Important?
business_purpose.content: Every production operation must be assigned to a Work Center. SAP uses Work Centers for scheduling operations, calculating capacity requirements, determining production costs and assigning manufacturing activities to the appropriate resources.

work_center_types.description: Used for packing and dispatch preparation.

capacity_planning.title: Capacity Planning
capacity_planning.content: Capacity Planning ensures that production workload is balanced with available machine and labor capacity. SAP compares required capacity from production orders with available capacity maintained in the Work Center.

integration.purpose: Every routing operation is assigned to a Work Center.
Production Orders use Work Centers for scheduling.
Work Centers are linked to Cost Centers for cost calculation.
Supports production scheduling and capacity checks.

business_example.title: Example
business_example.content: A Hot Rolling Mill in the manufacturing plant is maintained as a Work Center. During production planning, SAP assigns rolling operations to this Work Center, checks available machine capacity and schedules production accordingly.

best_practices: Assign every Work Center to the correct Cost Center.
important_notes: A Work Center can be used in multiple Routings.
Production scheduling depends on accurate Work Center data.
Capacity planning cannot function correctly without properly maintained Work Centers.
